limitations: Exact chat timestamps were not available for all messages in the thread history.
The log preserves several recent messages verbatim, but their timing is anchored between exact repo events rather than claimed as exact chat telemetry.

exactAnchors.message: player.release 0.6.11: restore modern_tv hosted interactive proof seam
player.dev_deploy 0.6.11 alpha: canonicalize modern_tv raw proof authority

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Restored the modern_tv hosted interactive proof seam and rebuilt the VaM-valid raw proof against the composed host package root.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: Defined strict deployment authority rules: .var or Custom, not both; every live file must be versioned; same semantic version cannot be reused for changed code; dev, prerelease, and release deploy labels must stay distinct.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: perfect. The previous one was before this scheme, so start with alpha, and give me a dev_deploy

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Canonicalized the live raw proof to alpha dev_deploy names and removed the older same-version raw direct artifacts so the modern_tv proof had one authority seam on disk.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: Raised a possible image-autoplay regression but explicitly held it as suspect because cached or mixed authority could still be polluting the test seam.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: Requested a versioned JSON running log of the operator conversation, retroactive when possible, because thread freezes and retrieval failures were wasting hours and threatening continuity.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: Future truth: the joystick scroller should be incorporated into the same naming, release, and deploy scheme as a prerelease candidate, but without letting that work distract from the current player conversation-log and proof task.

decisions: The live dev retest authority for 0.6.11 is the alpha raw Custom seam, not the .var seam.
Per-version operator conversation logs are now durable repo truth alongside changelogs and receipts.
Retroactive logs must clearly separate exact repo timestamps from reconstructed conversation timing.
openQuestions: Does the 0.6.11 alpha dev_deploy still show image autoplay when tested as the sole raw authority seam?
When the current player proof task is settled, how should the joystick scroller be carried forward as a prerelease candidate under the same naming canon?
notes: This is the first conversation log created under the formal canon.
Going forward, active version or deploy-iteration logs should be updated in-step with the changelog and receipts instead of reconstructed later.
